commit.name: Weather Card
commit.description: Data display with props, design tokens, and responsive layout
commit.prompt:
Build a weather card component. It should display:
- Current temperature (large, prominent)
- Weather condition with an appropriate icon/emoji (sunny, cloudy, rainy, etc.)
- Humidity percentage
- Wind speed
- A 5-day forecast strip at the bottom showing day name, icon, and high/low temps

All data must come from props — never hardcode weather data. Use CSS variables from the design system for all colors and spacing.

issues.description: CurrentWeather.tsx is truncated mid-implementation. Component rendering logic is incomplete, preventing full feature verification.
issues.fix: Complete the CurrentWeather component render method and verify all sub-components (CurrentWeather, WeatherDetails, ForecastStrip, ForecastCard) are fully implemented.

issues.description: Main WeatherCard component not provided. Cannot verify root component structure, props passing, or integration of sub-components.
issues.fix: Provide the main WeatherCard.tsx component that assembles all sub-components and calls useComponent hook.

issues.description: WeatherDetails and ForecastStrip/ForecastCard sub-components not provided. Cannot verify humidity/wind display or 5-day forecast rendering.
issues.fix: Provide implementations of WeatherDetails.tsx, ForecastStrip.tsx, and ForecastCard.tsx components.

issues.description: No styling/CSS provided. Cannot assess layout composition, spacing hierarchy, or visual polish. Design token usage cannot be verified in practice.
issues.fix: Provide CSS module or styled-components implementation showing how design tokens are applied to layout, spacing, and typography.

issues.description: DESIGN_TOKENS references specific CSS variables that may not exist in @ggui-ai/design. No fallback values or verification provided.
issues.fix: Either document that these CSS variables exist in the design system, or use actual token exports from @ggui-ai/design if available.

issues.description: No interactive states, transitions, or hover effects mentioned. Component appears to be static display only.
issues.fix: Add CSS transitions (200ms ease) to cards, implement hover states for forecast cards, and consider adding click handlers if forecast cards should be interactive.

issues.description: No loading state, error handling, or skeleton UI for when weather data is unavailable or being fetched.
issues.fix: Add optional isLoading, error props; implement loading indicators or skeleton UI.

issues.description: No ARIA labels provided for weather icons/emojis. Semantic structure (landmarks, headings hierarchy) cannot be assessed without full component code.
issues.fix: Add aria-label to temperature display and icons (e.g., aria-label='Sunny weather'), ensure Heading components use correct hierarchy (h2 for city, h3 for 'Humidity').

issues.description: Weather condition categorization and emoji mapping may not be descriptive enough for screen readers.
issues.fix: Render visible text label for condition (e.g., 'Sunny ☀️') rather than emoji alone, or add aria-label with full condition description.

issues.description: Wind speed formatted as 'm/s' hardcoded in hook, but no unit customization for imperial/metric preference.
issues.fix: Make wind speed units dynamic based on temperature unit preference (e.g., 'mph' for Fahrenheit, 'm/s' for Celsius).

issues.description: categorizeCondition function duplicates logic from CONDITION_CATEGORY_MAP constant. Inconsistent approach to condition mapping.
issues.fix: Refactor to use CONDITION_CATEGORY_MAP consistently; either lookup directly or use the helper function everywhere.

evaluations.critique: The code demonstrates solid architectural planning with well-defined type contracts, constants, and custom hooks. However, the implementation is incomplete—the main component and all sub-components are either missing or truncated, making it impossible to evaluate the actual UI rendering, layout polish, or interactivity. The hook logic is clean and properly handles data transformation, but without CSS and full component code, visual design, accessibility implementation, and polish cannot be verified. This appears to be ~40% of the final deliverable.
